publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

sourceDesc.p: Work of the literatures of Ethiopia and Eritrea
A TEI file converted from the ETHIO-authority google spreadsheet

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

assertion.unit: Sending of the letter 36 (ፍናዌ፡ መልእክት፡ ሠላሰ፡ ወስድስቱ።)
Homily on the manifestation of Jesus Christ in men, followed by a few sentences of a monk.
Homily on voluntary death (renunciation of the world) and the future life
Homily on the angels' love towards the monks, on the temptations of evil spirits, etc.
Homily on the gifts of the Spirit received by those who dedicate themselves to the contemplation of God
Homily on the constant contemplation of God
Homily on the different gifts of God
Homily on the contemplation of the Trinity
Homily on prayer, the virtue of spiritual natures, knowledge of God, etc.
Explanation of the vision narrated in
Homily on delight of those who love God while they contemplate his glory
Homily on the inappropriateness of speaking badly of the Fathers
Epistle written by a brother on the purification of the soul through penitence
Homily on the manifestation of God in the soul and of the soul's intimacy with God
Homily on fight and victory on the road to perfection
Homily on the intimacy (በእንተ፡ ስነ፡ ሞገስ፡) which the souls moved by the Spirit enjoy with God
Answer written by the author to his brother on the purification of the soul through penitence
Homily on the contemplation of divine providence
Homily on the mysteries of the New World
Homily on anger, insanity and pride
Homily on control of the inner and outer senses
Homily on the renunciation of the world and the cessation of intimacy
Letter by to a monk called John in Kanūbiyūn and the latter's answer
Answer from the monk John to
Subscription concerning the translation of the work
Letter by to a monk called John
Compendium of three homilies on the spiritual life / Dǝrsān baʾǝnta ʾarʾǝsta ʾaʾǝmro

t:abstract.t:p: ʾAragāwi manfasāwi, "the spritual elder" is in Ethiopian tradition both the name of the author John Saba and of his anthology of homilies and letters, which was translated from Syriac to Arabic and from Arabic to Gǝʿǝz.

The anthology contains in Syriac 25-28 homilies, 48-51 letters and 3-8 homilies under the title "Chapters on Knowledge". The Arabic and Gǝʿǝz version have 30 homilies, 48 letters and 3 "Chapters on Knowledge" and as a preface or epilogue a letter from John Saba to another monk and the latter's answer. Geschichte der christlichen arabischen Literatur<hi rend="italics">, I:</hi> Die ÜbersetzungenStudi e TestiGeorgGrafCittà del VaticanoBiblioteca Apostolica Vaticana1944Studi e Testi118I118https://archive.org/details/GGrafGeschichtebm:Graf1944GCALIbm:Graf1944GCALI434-436

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

relation.name: saws:isVersionInAnotherLanguageOf
relation.t:desc: The work was translated in the 16th century on the order of Lǝbna Dǝngǝl, according to Ethiopian tradition by Mārqos [I] and ʾƎnbāqom. Arägawi mänfäsawiEAeEncyclopaedia AethiopicaEnzoLucchesiSiegbertUhligWiesbadenHarrassowitz Verlag2003I309b-310abm:Lucchesi2003Aragawibm:Lucchesi2003Aragawi

relation.name: saws:isVersionInAnotherLanguageOf
relation.t:desc: The Syriac original was translated to Arabic before the middle of the 13th century by a deacon called Yuḥannā and a priest called ʾĪbrāhīm. Geschichte der christlichen arabischen Literatur<hi rend="italics">, I:</hi> Die ÜbersetzungenStudi e TestiGeorgGrafCittà del VaticanoBiblioteca Apostolica Vaticana1944Studi e Testi118I118https://archive.org/details/GGrafGeschichtebm:Graf1944GCALIbm:Graf1944GCALI434

t:bibl.title: Geschichte der christlichen arabischen Literatur<hi rend="italics">, I:</hi> Die Übersetzungen
Studi e Testi

Bibliotheca Orientalis Clementino-Vaticana: In Qua Manuscriptos Codices Syriacos, Arabicos, Persicos, Turcicos, Hebraicos, Samaritanos, Armenicos, Aethiopicos, Graecos, Aegyptiacos, Ibericos, & Malabaricos, Jussu Et Munificentia Clementis XI. Pontificis Maximi Ex Oriente conquisitos, comparatos, avectos, & Bibliothecae Vaticanae addictos
t:bibl.publisher: Typis Sacræ Congregationis de Propaganda Fide

t:bibl.title: Catalogues des manuscrits syriaques et sabéens (mandaïtes) de la Bibliothèque nationale
Manuscrits orientaux
Maṣaḥǝfta manakosāt. Śostǝññā maṣḥaf. ʾAragāwi manfasāwi

La version éthiopienne de la collection des lettres de Jean de Dalyatha dit «Aragawi Manfasawi»: Édition critique du texte éthiopien (guèze) inédit, comparaison avec la version arabe, traduction française, étude

t:div.t:label: Incipit
t:div.t:note: The following text is taken from Paris, Bibliothèque nationale de France, BnF Éthiopien 115.
t:div.t:ab: እወጥን፡ ጽሒፈ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ዘአረጋዊ፡ ጻድቅ፡ መንፈሳዊ። ዘይቤ፡ በእንተ፡ ሀብታት፡ አምላካውያት፡ ወሕዋጼ፡ ጸጋ፡ እንተ፡ ይፌንዋ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ለኑዛዜ፡ ባሕታውያን፡ ወበተድላሆሙ፡ ወበእንተ፡ ራእያት፡ መንፈሳዊያት፡ ወአእምሮ፡ ኅቡአት፡ ወበእንተ፡ ሕሊናት፡ ወጸብአ፡ ሰይጣናት፡ ወተጋድሎ፡ ወዘይከውን፡ ምስሌሆሙ፡ በእንተ፡ ፈሊጠ፡ ምግባር፡ ዘሠናይ፡ ወዘእኩይ። በእንተ፡ ሥርዓታት። ወምግባር፡ ትሩፋት፡ ወጸሎታት። ወዓዲ፡ በእንተ፡ አእምሮ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ወምሥጢራተ፡ ዓለም፡ ሐዲስ።

t:div.t:label: Prayers preceding the Homilies
t:div.t:note: The following text is taken from Vatican City, Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana, Aeth. 84.
t:div.t:ab: ጸሎት፡ ኦክርስቶስ፡ ወልደ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ሕያው፡ ክሥት፡ አዕይንተ፡ አልባቢነ፡ ከመ፡ ንለቡ፡ አንብቦ፡ መጻሕፍቲከ፡ ቅዱሳት፡ አሜን፨ አንተ፡ በኃይልከ፡ ዘታነሥኦሙ፡ ለኵሎሙ፡ ጠባይዕት፡ ነባብያን፡ ሀበነ፡ ለነኒ፡ ኃይለ፡ ለድኩማን፡ ወለኅጡኣነ፡ አእምሮ፡ ከመ፡ ንጽሐፍ፡ ወጸግወነ፡ መንፈሰከ፡ ከመ፡ ንቅልም። በጸሎታ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ንጽሕት፡ ወጸሎተ፡ ኵሎሙ፡ እለ፡ አሥመሩ፡ ፈቃደከ፡ አሜን፨

t:div.t:label: Homily on self control
t:div.t:note: On Alertness and Vigilance and on Detachment from Passions (cp. Ms. BN éth. 148, a.; Ms. Cod.Vat.aethiop. 84, 1; Ms. EMML no. 1937, 1.)

t:div.t:label: Homily on fight and victory on the road to perfection
t:div.t:note: On Being Granted Wisdom of the Heart of the Holy Spirit and on Light for the Alert [which gives] Joy (cp. Ms. BN éth. 148, b.; Ms. Cod.Vat.aethiop. 84,2; Ms. EMML no. 1937, 2.)

t:div.t:label: 2nd part
t:div.t:note: On Spiritual Visions Which Are Granted to Hermits for their Contemplation

t:div.t:label: 3rd part
t:div.t:note: The Beginning Stage of Contemplation

t:div.t:label: 4th part
t:div.t:note: The Intermediate Stage

t:div.t:label: 5th part
t:div.t:note: The Stage of Perfection

t:div.t:label: Homily on fornication
t:div.t:note: On Fornication

t:div.t:label: Homily on blasphemy
t:div.t:note: On Blasphemy

t:div.t:label: Homily on anger, insanity and pride
t:div.t:note: On Anger, On Distraction, On Vainglory

t:div.t:label: Homily on the angels' love towards the monks, on the temptations of evil spirits, etc.
t:div.t:note: On Love which Angels Reveal to the Pure in Heart and the Vigilant and the Repentant

t:div.t:label: Homily on true spritual visions
t:div.t:note: The Arabic title is ميمر على الثاوريا القاطعة, where according to Hermann Zotenberg, ṯāwirīya should be translated with "contemplation" instead of "theory".

t:bibl.title: Catalogues des manuscrits syriaques et sabéens (mandaïtes) de la Bibliothèque nationale
Manuscrits orientaux

t:div.t:label: Homily on the inappropriateness of speaking badly of the Fathers
t:div.t:note: On That [it should be] Undesirable for Anyone to Despise Holy Fathers When They Speak with Naivety in Accordance to the Minds of the Listeners

t:div.t:label: Homily on novices
t:div.t:note: On Brothers Who Have Begun (Novices) the Pure Rule Which Is Loved by God

t:div.t:label: Homily on prayer, the virtue of spiritual natures, knowledge of God, etc.
t:div.t:note: On Prayer and on Virtue of Spiritual Dispositions and on Knowledge of God

t:div.t:label: Homily on control of the inner and outer senses
t:div.t:note: On Guarding of Outer and Inner Senses and on Anything [What Should] Be Guarded

t:div.t:label: Homily on voluntary death (renunciation of the world) and the future life
t:div.t:note: On Death Which Comes from Desire of This World

t:div.t:label: Homily on the intimacy (በእንተ፡ ስነ፡ ሞገስ፡) which the souls moved by the Spirit enjoy with God
t:div.t:note: On Grace Which Is Acquired from God

t:div.t:label: Homily on the different gifts of God
t:div.t:note: On Gifts of Knowledge Which Are Granted by Grace from God

t:div.t:label: Homily on the mysteries of the New World
t:div.t:note: On Mysteries of the New World

t:div.t:label: Question to ʾAragāwi manfasāwi
t:div.t:note: A question of one brother to ʾAragāwi manfasāwi

t:div.t:label: Homily on the manifestation of God in the soul and of the soul's intimacy with God
t:div.t:note: On Admiration of the Majesty of God and Its Manifestation

t:div.t:label: Homily on the contemplation of the Trinity
t:div.t:note: On the Holy Trinity

t:div.t:label: Homily on the gifts of the Spirit received by those who dedicate themselves to the contemplation of God
t:div.t:note: On Divine Gifts of the Holy Spirit Which Are Given by Contemplation of God

t:div.t:label: Homily on delight of those who love God while they contemplate his glory
t:div.t:note: On the Sweetness of Comfort Which Moves Hearts of the Lovers of God

t:div.t:label: Homily on the renunciation of the world and the cessation of intimacy
t:div.t:note: On the Flight from the World and Detachment form Anxiety

t:div.t:label: Exhortation to penitence
t:div.t:note: On Alertness and Prudence, and On Penitence

t:div.t:label: Homily on the manifestation of Jesus Christ in men, followed by a few sentences of a monk.
t:div.t:note: On Revelation of Christ Our God

t:div.t:label: Epistle written by a brother on the purification of the soul through penitence
t:div.t:note: A reply to one of the brothers

t:div.t:label: Answer written by the author to his brother on the purification of the soul through penitence
t:div.t:note: A reply of ʾAragāwi manfasāwi to one brother concerning penitence which purifies the soul

t:div.t:label: Account of a vision
t:div.t:note: Vision That Appeared To One Man When He Approached to Perform Divine Mysteries

t:div.t:label: Explanation of the vision narrated in
t:div.t:note: On the Explanation [of the Visions] of the Previous Father

t:div.t:label: Homily on silence
t:div.t:note: On How the Toil Becomes Beneficial for the Vigilant

t:div.t:label: Homily on love of God
t:div.t:note: On God’s Love and From Where Begins Love Upon a Man

t:div.t:label: Saying of a monk
t:div.t:note: A reply of ʾAragāwi manfasāwi to a brother

t:div.t:label: Homily on the contemplation of divine providence
t:div.t:note: On Visions Given by God

t:div.t:label: Homily on seeing God
t:div.t:note: On Seeing God Which Is Made Possible for the Man Thanks to His Might

t:div.t:label: Homily on the constant contemplation of God
t:div.t:note: On Praising in Songs and [with] Zeal for Remembering about God

t:div.t:label: Epistles
t:div.t:note: in ESgkm001: 22 malʾǝkt and 27 fǝnnāwe malʾǝkt
in ESmakm012: 22 malʾǝkt and 28 fǝnnāwe malʾǝkt

t:div.t:label: Sending of the letter 36 (ፍናዌ፡ መልእክት፡ ሠላሰ፡ ወስድስቱ።)
Compendium of three homilies on the spiritual life / Dǝrsān baʾǝnta ʾarʾǝsta ʾaʾǝmro
t:div.t:note: The Syriac mss. contain Rêšê dîḏaʽṯâ i.e., “Chapters of Knowledge” (Beulay 1978: 262) which were rendered into Gǝʿǝz as ʾArʾǝsta ʾaʾǝmro (“Chapters of Knowledge” or “Heads of Knowledge”). In front of the title, the Ethiopian translator added "dǝrsān baʾǝnta" and retained the plural form of "ʾarʾǝst". For that reason in some catalogues of Ethiopic mss. a translation “Homilies on the heads of knowledge” may be encountered (s. e.g., BLorient 529, EMML 1937, EMML 2838, EMML 4043).

t:div.t:label: Letter by John Saba to a monk called John in Kanūbiyūn and the latter's answer
t:div.t:note: Letter of the saint Elder to his brother who was in a coenobitic Monastery (Malʾǝkta manfasāwi qǝddus ḫaba ʾǝḫuhu za-hallo yǝnabbǝr ba-dabra Konobyos)

t:bibl.title: Bibliotheca Orientalis Clementino-Vaticana: In Qua Manuscriptos Codices Syriacos, Arabicos, Persicos, Turcicos, Hebraicos, Samaritanos, Armenicos, Aethiopicos, Graecos, Aegyptiacos, Ibericos, & Malabaricos, Jussu Et Munificentia Clementis XI. Pontificis Maximi Ex Oriente conquisitos, comparatos, avectos, & Bibliothecae Vaticanae addictos
t:bibl.publisher: Typis Sacræ Congregationis de Propaganda Fide

t:bibl.title: La lettre et la notice finales du Vieillard Spirituel
Revue de l’Orient Chrétien
La lettre et la notice finales du Vieillard Spirituel
Revue de l’Orient Chrétien

t:div.t:label: Letter by John Saba to a monk called John
t:div.t:note: Petition (?) of the Saint Elder’s Own Brother in Which He Explains What Was the Reason for Writing the Book (Sǝʾǝlata ʾǝḫuhu la-qǝddus ʾAragāwi)

t:div.t:label: Answer from the monk John to John Saba
Subscription concerning the translation of the work
t:div.t:ab: The following text is taken from ʾƎndartā, Māy ʾAnbasā Dabra Gannat Kidāna Mǝḥrat, MAKM-012.ተተርጐመ፡ ዝንቱ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ እምልሳነ፡ ዓረቢ፡ ኀበ፡ ልሳነ፡ ግዕዝ፡ ሐዲስ፡ በትእዛዘ፡ ቃሉ፡ ለልብነ፡ ድንግል፡ ንጉሥ፨ ይጽሐፍ፡ ስሞ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ በሰሌዳ፡ ስሙ፡ ለአረጋዊ፡ ምሉአ፡ መንፈስ አሜን። ተፈጸመት፡ ዛቲ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ በ፸፻፡ በ፲ወ፯፡ እምአመተ፡ ኵነኔ፡ በሐሳበ፡ ግብጻውያን፡ ፸፻ ፲ወ፬፡ በሐሳበ፡ ጽርእ፡ ወሮማውያን፡ በ፲፻ ወ፭፻፲ወ፯ እምልደተ፡ መድኅን፡ በ፲፻ ወ፵ ዓመተ፡ ሰማዕት፡ መዋዕያን፡ አመ፡ ኃሙስ፡ ለወርኃ፡ መሰራ፡ በሐሳበ፡ ያዕቆባውያን፡ ወአመ፡ ፳፱ ለተሙዝ፡ በሐሳበ፡ ዕብራውያን፡ ዝንዮ፡ በዮናናውያን፡ ዝውእቱ፡ ሐምሌ፡ ወአመ፡ ሐሙሱ፡ ለነኅሴ፡ በሐሳበ፡ ኢትዮጵያውያን። እንዘ፡ አበቅቴ፡ ወርኃ፡ ፲ወ፬፡ ወአበቅቴ፡ ፀሐይ፡ ፭ ለኅእሊሚሞከ፡ በዘመነ፡ እብሬቱ፡ ወእንዘ፡ ሀሎ፡ ፀሐይ፡ በሳድስ፡ ኆኅቱ፡ ወበ፳፡ ወተ፱መዐርጋቱ፡ ስብሐት፡ ወክብር፡ ይደሉ፡ ለዘያስተባሪ፡ ምዕዋይ፡ ዘመናት። ግናይ፡ ወሰጊድ፡ ለወላዲተ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን። ኦኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ አጽፈ፡ ኢያደልዎ፡ ለባሴ፡ ስረይ፡ ሊተ፡ ለገብርከ፡ {አእምሮ} ነዳየ፡ ፈላሲ፡ ዘአሰለውኩ፡ ዘንተ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ እምነ፡ ዓረቢ፡ ለአባሲ፡ ድካመ፡ ጻማየ፡ በከ፡ ኢትረሲ፡ በእነተ፡ ወላዲትከ፡ መድኃተ፡ ኀጥእ፡ ወተስፋ፡ አባሲ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን፡ ለይኩን፡ ለይኩን።

The following text is taken from Gāntā ʾAfašum, Maḵodʿa Dabra Gannat Kidāna Mǝḥrat, GKM-001.ተተርጐመ፡ ዝንቱ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ እምልሳነ፡ ዓረቢ፡ ኅበ፡ ልሳነ፡ ግዕዝ፡ ሐዲስ፡ በትእዛዘ፡ ቃሉ፡ ለልብነ፡ ድንግል፡ ንጉሥ፨ ወለዛቲ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ አጽሐፍክዋ፡ አነ [vac.] በእንተ፡ ፍቅሩ፡ ለኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፨ እምቃለ፡ ኑዛዜ፡ እንተ፡ ዘአረጋዊ፡ መንፈሳዊ፡ በእንተ፡ አሥምሮተ፡ እግዚአብሔር፨ ወምሥጢራተ፡ ዓለም፡ ሐዲስ፨ ወበእንተ፡ ሀብታት፡ አምላካውያት፨ ወሕዋጼ፡ ጸጋ፡ እንተ፡ ይፌንዋ፡ ሎሙ፡ ለእለ፡ ይፈቅርዎ፡ ፍቁራኒሁ፡ ለኑዛዜሆሙ፡ ወተድላሆሙ፡ ለርእየ፡ ዚአሁ፥ እስከ፡ ይረስዑ፡ ርእሶሙ፡ ወይሠጠሙ፡ ውስተ፡ ተደሞ፥ ዘባሕርየ፡ አስተርእዮቱ፡ ብርሃናዊ፨ ቡሩክ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ዘወሀበ፡ እሎንተ፡ በዝየ፡ ለጠባይዓቲነ፨ ሎቱ፡ ስብሐት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን፨
